reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): The code at consensus/types/src/chain_spec.rs:828-830 is factually correct. When blob_retention_epoch > fulu_fork_epoch, line 829 returns current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS without applying max(result, FULU_FORK_EPOCH). This creates a window during the first 4096 epochs after Fulu fork where the boundary can be set below FULU_FORK_EPOCH. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P DataColumnsByRangeRequest during the vulnerable window (when fulu_fork_epoch < current_epoch < fulu_fork_epoch + 4096), requesting data for epochs before FULU_FORK_EPOCH where data columns should not exist. The attack path is attacker-triggered via untrusted P2P entry point. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): The spec requirement is documented in outputs/graphs/W1B2_1770278556/fulu-p2p-interface/SG-008_data_column_sidecars_by_range.mmd line 7 and INV-006 line 31: 'data_column_serve_range = [max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH), current_epoch]'. The 01e property text in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:752 states 'data_column_serve_range lower bound must be max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH)'. Code violates this requirement by omitting the max() in the post-fork branch. Severity: Medium severity is appropriate. Impact is limited to the 4096-epoch transition window. Nodes would accept invalid requests but respond with ResourceUnavailable (no data exists pre-fork), causing inconsistent sync state and potential DoS against syncing nodes during the transition period. Does not reach High threshold (>33% network impact) as it's time-limited and self-resolving after the window closes.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_serve_range lower bound must be max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH).
